title: Lomatium humile
intro: Lomatium humile, also known as small Lomatium or small biscuitroot, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The bioactive constituents of Lomatium humile include a range of alkaloids, including sesquiterpenes, furanocoumarins, and benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lomatium humile are tinctures and teas, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health issues.
taxonomy: Lomatium humile is classified in the family Apiaceae, which is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This plant is further classified in the genus Lomatium and is a type of perennial herb. Lomatium humile is native to the western United States and is also known as small lomatium. It is a small plant, usually growing to be around 10-20 centimeters in height, with blue-green foliage and small yellow flowers. The classification of Lomatium humile is a part of its identification and is used by botanists and horticulturists to study and understand the plant's characteristics.
uses: Lomatium humile, a plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Its roots and stems contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as digestive problems.
